title: inula acaulis subsp. acaulis
intro: Inula acaulis subsp. acaulis, also known as the stemless elecampane, is a perennial plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its inulin-rich roots, which have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Inula acaulis subsp. acaulis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the roots of the plant can be dried and used as a tea or added to other remedies, such as tinctures and infusions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
